codeReviewIssues.description: Timeout assertions in tests use OR (network_timeout is not None or connection_timeout is not None) rather than AND. Since _create_triton_http_client sets both kwargs, an AND assertion would be stricter and ensure both timeouts are always forwarded. The current OR technically matches the feature description wording but provides weaker coverage.

The 5-second timeout constants (TRITON_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, TRITON_NETWORK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) are hardcoded module-level values with no way to override via config, constructor, or environment variable. This is acceptable for the hardening fix but may need configurability if different deployment environments require different timeout thresholds.

sharedStateObservations.description: The worker discovered that tritonclient.http.InferenceServerClient accepts connection_timeout and network_timeout constructor kwargs (both floats, in seconds). This API knowledge could be recorded in .factory/library/ so future workers modifying Triton client usage don't need to re-discover it via web search or signature introspection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows a WebSearch call to docs.nvidia.com and an inspect.signature() call to verify the constructor parameters before implementing the helper.

summary: Clean implementation that correctly centralizes Triton HTTP client construction in a _create_triton_http_client helper with explicit 5-second connection and network timeouts, then reuses it from both is_available() and classify(). All three required edge-case tests (deps unavailable, client creation failure, server not live) are present and correct. Existing tests were enhanced to assert timeout kwargs are forwarded. The worker followed TDD procedure, ran all local gates (ruff, mypy, full pytest suite), and committed on a clean tree. No blocking issues found.
